features.featureTitle: In the Couple Details step (CoupleDetailsStep.tsx) and the Invitation Editor's couple details tab (InvitationEditor.tsx), replace the single 'couple photo URL' input field with two separate file upload inputs — one labeled 'Bride Photo' and one labeled 'Groom Photo'. Each upload should allow the user to select an image file, show a preview thumbnail, and store the uploaded photo independently. The InvitationFormContext must be updated to hold separate bridePhoto and groomPhoto fields instead of a single couplePhoto field. The backend Invitation type in main.mo must be updated to store bridePhoto and groomPhoto as separate optional Text fields. The guest-facing invitation pages (HeroSection, CoupleIntroduction) must render both photos side by side where a couple photo is currently displayed.

On the Dashboard page (Dashboard.tsx), fix the layout so that invitation management controls — including the Publish button and any similar action buttons — remain fully visible and accessible at all times, including while an invitation is in an editing state. No action button should be hidden, clipped, or obscured based on editing state.

Remove or conditionally hide the 'Create Invitation' call-to-action text/button from pages where it does not belong. It should only appear on the Landing page and Dashboard page. It must NOT appear inside the invitation editor, the create wizard steps, or the guest-facing invitation page. Also fix the Header component (Header.tsx) so its 'Create Invitation' CTA does not render on editor or wizard routes, preventing it from overlapping other page sections.
